success.description: True if AND only if: README found + install section located + mcp_type ∈ {stdio,http} + corresponding config non-empty + valid name + confidence ∈ {high,medium}. False on any failure mode.

mcp_type.description: Transport. 'unknown' only when Step 2 cannot decide; in that case success MUST be false.

name.description: Sanitized MCP name. Must match [A-Za-z0-9_.-]+, cannot start with . or -, cannot contain .., max 64 chars. Empty string allowed only when success=false.

command.description: Stdio executable (e.g. 'npx', 'uvx', 'docker', 'python'). MUST be null when mcp_type='http' or 'unknown'.

args.description: Stdio command arguments in order. Empty array when mcp_type='http' or 'unknown'.

url.description: HTTP endpoint URL. May contain {NAME} placeholders for user-supplied values. MUST be null when mcp_type='stdio' or 'unknown'. MUST NOT be empty string when mcp_type='http'.

headers.description: HTTP headers the user must supply. Empty array when mcp_type='stdio' or 'unknown'.

is_secret.description: TRUE for any header whose name contains Authorization, Token, Api-Key, Secret. Use empty default ('' or 'Bearer ' template) for secrets — never embed a real token.

default.description: Literal template if present (e.g. 'Bearer '); empty string '' if user must supply. Never a real secret.

required_env_vars.description: For stdio: required environment variables (from Source A/B/C fallback). For http: URL {NAME} placeholder variables. Empty array if none required.

is_secret.description: TRUE if name contains KEY, TOKEN, SECRET, PASSWORD, CREDENTIAL, API (case-insensitive), even if README does not explicitly state.

default.description: Literal default from .env.example RHS if non-secret AND non-placeholder; otherwise empty string ''. NEVER 'your-key-here' or similar.

notes.description: Provenance + caveats (≤ 280 chars). On success: which README section + which env source (A/B/C). On failure: specific blocker. Always required, never empty.

confidence.description: high=Step 2 Rule 1/2 + Step 3 Source A. medium=Step 2 Rule 3/4 + Step 3 Source B. low=Step 2 Rule 5 OR placeholders OR guesses. success=true REQUIRES high or medium.
